skill.name: parallel-workflows
skill.description: Helps AI agents plan independent work in parallel, coordinate task agents, and summarize combined results safely.
skill.summary: Plan, run, and report independent AI workflow tasks in parallel.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: Static Ruby backtick and weak-crypto findings are false positives from Markdown code fences, examples, and frontmatter text. The skill contains no executable script, network access, secret access, or prompt injection attempt, but it does discuss local command examples and parallel task execution.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Detections Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown fences, inline command examples, or report templates. No executable Ruby code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious characters are Markdown code block delimiters and template placeholders. The file is documentation only, with no Ruby runtime or script entry point.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detection Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography match at the description line has no cryptographic API, hash operation, cipher, or key handling evidence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains skill description metadata and trigger phrases. I found no evidence of weak cryptographic algorithm usage in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Detection Is Caused By Mixed Language Documentation
low_findings.description: The entropy heuristic appears to be triggered by Japanese text, Markdown tables, symbols, and examples. No binary, encrypted payload, or obfuscated code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file is readable Markdown content. The structure and examples are visible, and no hidden payload or decoding path is present.

low_findings.title: Local Command Examples Require Normal Execution Controls
low_findings.description: The skill recommends commands such as lint, type-check, test, and build. These are legitimate examples but should still follow user approval and workspace policy.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are ordinary project quality checks, not suspicious payloads. Risk remains low because running local commands can have side effects in some repositories.

content.value_statement: Complex AI work slows down when independent tasks run one after another. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code split safe tasks, run them in parallel, and combine results.
actual_capabilities: Identifies tasks that can run without file, data, or order dependencies.
Guides the use of multiple task agents for independent analysis or implementation work.
Suggests parallel quality checks such as lint, type checks, and tests before build steps.
Provides report formats for combining task status, timing, outputs, and changed files.
Documents failure handling when some parallel tasks succeed and others fail.
limitations: Does not execute tasks by itself; it provides workflow guidance for an AI agent.
Requires careful review before parallel edits touch nearby or shared files.
Should not parallelize migrations, dependent implementation steps, or ordered release actions.
Performance examples are illustrative and may not match every repository.

use_cases.title: Speed Up Project Analysis
use_cases.description: Split repository review into structure, dependency, and quality checks, then merge the findings into one summary.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Independent UI Work
use_cases.description: Assign separate page or component tasks to parallel agents when each task owns different files.

use_cases.title: Run Efficient Quality Checks
use_cases.description: Plan lint, type checking, and tests as parallel checks before a sequential build or release step.

prompt_templates.title: Find Parallel Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this task list and identify which items can run in parallel. Explain any dependencies that require sequential work.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze A Repository
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this project in parallel across structure, dependencies, code quality, and test coverage. Return one combined report.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Independent Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement these independent features in parallel only when they do not edit the same files. Report conflicts before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Design A Parallel Execution Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a parallel execution plan for these tasks. Include dependency groups, agent assignments, validation steps, and merge criteria.

output_examples.input: Analyze the project structure, dependencies, and code quality.
output_examples.output: A combined report that separates structure findings, dependency findings, and quality findings, with timing and follow-up actions.

output_examples.input: Build the header, footer, and sidebar components.
output_examples.output: A task report showing three independent component assignments, completion status, changed files, and any integration notes.

output_examples.input: Run all checks before I merge this change.
output_examples.output: A validation summary that runs independent checks first, then recommends build or release only after required checks pass.

best_practices: Confirm file ownership before starting parallel implementation tasks.
Run dependent build or release steps only after parallel checks finish.
Merge agent results into one concise report with failures and changed files.
anti_patterns: Do not parallelize tasks that write to the same file.
Do not run database migrations or release actions in parallel.
Do not hide failed subtask results inside a successful summary.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance. Any command execution depends on the host AI tool and user permissions.

faq.question: Can it edit multiple files at once?
faq.answer: It can guide parallel agents to edit independent files. Shared files should be handled sequentially.

faq.question: Is this useful for non-coding work?
faq.answer: Yes. It can split independent research, documentation, or review tasks when outputs do not depend on each other.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When should I avoid it?
faq.answer: Avoid it when tasks have order dependencies, shared write targets, database changes, or release side effects.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk low?
faq.answer: The skill is documentation only. It includes command examples, but no executable script, network access, or obfuscated payload was found.
